Title: Infrastructure Setup (CP+DP+K8s+PostgreSQL)
Description: Verify CP Admin API, DP proxy flow, K8s deployment, and PostgreSQL persistence
Test Steps:
1. kubectl get pods - verify CP and DP running
2. Hit CP Admin API - verify DB connected
3. Deploy a service+route - send request via DP
4. Verify demo server receives proxied request
5. Restart CP pod - check config persists
6. Check K8s service endpoints
Expected Results: Both CP and DP healthy. Traffic flows. Config persists across CP restart.
Feature Checklist: HA Support, Container Support (K8s), REST Support, Backend Protocol (HTTP)

Title: Upstream Load Balancing
Description: Verify Kong distributes requests across multiple upstream targets
Test Steps:
1. Configure upstream with 2 demo server instances
2. Send 10 requests
3. Check round-robin distribution across targets
Expected Results: Requests distributed round-robin across all healthy upstream targets.

Title: HA Failover
Description: Verify Kong fails over when one upstream target goes down
Test Steps:
1. Configure upstream with 2 instances
2. Take one target down
3. Continue sending requests
4. Verify 0 dropped requests, remaining target handles all traffic
Expected Results: 0 dropped requests. Traffic routes to remaining healthy target only.

Title: API Key Authentication
Description: Verify Kong key-auth plugin enforces API key on routes
Test Steps:
1. Enable key-auth plugin on route
2. Create consumer with API key
3. Test: valid key -> 200, invalid key -> 401, no key -> 401
Expected Results: Valid key 200, invalid 401, missing 401.

Title: JWT Validation
Description: Verify Kong jwt plugin validates RS256 JWT tokens
Test Steps:
1. Enable jwt plugin on route
2. Generate RS256 JWT with proper claims
3. Test: valid JWT -> 200, expired -> 401, tampered -> 401
Expected Results: Valid JWT 200, expired/tampered 401/403.

Title: JWT Scope Enforcement
Description: Verify Kong enforces scope-based access via JWT claims
Test Steps:
1. Issue JWT with scope=read_only and scope=write
2. Test read_only: GET -> 200, POST -> 403
3. Test write scope: GET+POST -> both 200
Expected Results: read_only: read allowed, write denied. write: both allowed.

Title: mTLS Client Certificate
Description: Verify mTLS authentication for client certificate auth
Test Steps:
1. Enable mTLS on route
2. Generate test client cert
3. Test: valid cert -> 200, expired cert -> 401, no cert -> 401
Expected Results: Valid cert 200, invalid/no cert 401.

Title: IP Whitelisting
Description: Verify Kong enforces IP-based access restrictions
Test Steps:
1. Configure IP restriction plugin (allow specific CIDR)
2. Send from allowed IP -> 200
3. Send from blocked IP -> 403
Expected Results: Allowed IP 200, blocked IP 403.

Title: Deny-by-default
Description: Verify unmatched routes return 403
Test Steps:
1. Delete all routes from Kong
2. Hit any path against DP
3. Verify returns 403
Expected Results: Unmatched routes return 403.

Title: CORS Configuration
Description: Verify Kong handles CORS preflight requests and origin validation
Test Steps:
1. Enable CORS plugin on a route
2. Send OPTIONS from allowed origin -> correct headers
3. Send from disallowed origin -> no CORS headers
Expected Results: CORS headers correct for allowed origins. Disallowed origins rejected.

Title: Rate Limiting
Description: Verify per-consumer rate limiting
Test Steps:
1. Enable rate-limiting plugin (5 req/min per consumer)
2. Send 7 requests as same consumer
3. First 5 -> 200, 6th+7th -> 429
4. Wait 1 min - verify limit resets
Expected Results: First 5 -> 200. 6th+7th -> 429. Limit resets after cooldown.

Title: Quota Management
Description: Verify route-level daily quota across all consumers
Test Steps:
1. Configure route-level quota plugin (daily 500 req)
2. Multiple consumers hit same route
3. Exceed daily aggregate quota
4. Verify 429 returned
Expected Results: Aggregate quota across all consumers. Exceed -> 429. Resets daily.

Title: Throttling
Description: Verify sustained traffic smoothing
Test Steps:
1. Configure rate-limiting with sustained=2/sec
2. Send sustained traffic at 5/sec
3. Observe actual throughput rate
Expected Results: Traffic smoothed to configured rate. Excess requests queued or rejected.

Title: Spike Arrest
Description: Verify burst control
Test Steps:
1. Configure rate-limiting with burst=10
2. Send 20 requests in 1 second
3. Observe throughput - first 10 pass, remaining limited
Expected Results: First 10 pass immediately (burst). Remaining requests throttled.

Title: Circuit Breaker
Description: Verify Kong opens circuit when backend repeatedly fails
Test Steps:
1. Configure circuit breaker (5 failures / 30s)
2. Make demo server unresponsive
3. Send multiple requests
4. After threshold -> circuit opens -> 503
5. Cooldown -> half-open -> retry -> circuit closes
Expected Results: Circuit opens after 5 failures -> 503. Cooldown -> half-open -> retry.

Title: Backend Retry
Description: Verify Kong retries failed requests before giving up
Test Steps:
1. Configure retry plugin (2 attempts)
2. Make demo server intermittently fail
3. Send request
4. Verify Kong retries before returning error
Expected Results: Kong retries configured number of times before returning error.

Title: Response Caching
Description: Verify Kong caches responses to reduce backend load
Test Steps:
1. Enable proxy-cache plugin
2. Send same GET request twice
3. Compare latency and X-Cache-Status header
Expected Results: First: Miss. Second: Hit, lower latency, X-Cache-Status: Hit.

Title: Access Logging
Description: Verify Kong logs all proxy requests with complete fields
Test Steps:
1. Enable http-log plugin (stdout or file)
2. Send several test requests
3. Check log fields: timestamp, IP, method, path, status, latency, consumer_id
Expected Results: Complete request log for every request with all required fields.

Title: Correlation ID Tracing
Description: Verify end-to-end trace via correlation ID header
Test Steps:
1. Enable correlation-id plugin
2. Send request with custom correlation ID header
3. Check same ID appears in Kong logs
Expected Results: Correlation ID propagates through Kong and appears in access logs.

Title: Prometheus Metrics
Description: Verify Kong exposes Prometheus metrics
Test Steps:
1. Enable prometheus plugin
2. Hit /metrics endpoint
3. Verify fields: requests_total, latency_ms, status codes, connections, memory
Expected Results: Metrics available at /metrics with request count, latency histogram, status breakdown.

Title: Alerting
Description: Verify Kong alerts when error rate exceeds threshold
Test Steps:
1. Configure alert threshold on error rate > 5%
2. Trigger 50% errors (make backend fail)
3. Verify alert triggers
Expected Results: Alert triggers when error rate exceeds configured threshold.

Title: Path-based Routing
Description: Verify Kong routes based on request path
Test Steps:
1. Configure /api/demo1 -> endpoint A
2. Configure /api/demo2 -> endpoint B
3. Send requests to each path - verify correct backend
Expected Results: Each path routes to its correct backend endpoint.

Title: Host-based Routing
Description: Verify Kong routes based on Host header for multi-tenant support
Test Steps:
1. Configure api.fubon.com -> backend A
2. Configure portal.fubon.com -> backend B
3. Send with different Host headers - verify correct backend
Expected Results: Each hostname routes to correct backend. Supports multi-tenant routing.

Title: Request Transformation
Description: Verify Kong transforms headers and body before forwarding
Test Steps:
1. Enable header-transformation plugin
2. Add, remove, modify request headers
3. Verify backend receives modified headers
Expected Results: Headers modified as configured before reaching backend.

Title: Backend Protocol (HTTPS/TCP)
Description: Verify Kong routes to HTTPS and TCP backends
Test Steps:
1. Configure route to HTTPS backend with TLS cert
2. Verify TLS termination works
3. Configure route to TCP backend (non-HTTP)
4. Verify raw TCP proxied correctly
Expected Results: HTTPS: TLS terminated at Kong. TCP: raw TCP proxied correctly.

Title: API Versioning
Description: Verify multiple API versions coexist and deprecated versions removed safely
Test Steps:
1. Deploy /v1/accounts and /v2/accounts with different backends
2. Call both versions - both serve correctly
3. Deprecate v1 route - v2 still works
Expected Results: Both versions serve correctly. v1 deprecated without breaking v2.

Title: GitOps Config Management (decK)
Description: Verify declarative config via decK: export, validate, sync, rollback
Test Steps:
1. Export Kong config via decK to YAML
2. Modify YAML - add new route
3. Run deck validate - catches syntax errors
4. Run deck sync - import config
5. Apply broken config - deck validate rejects
6. Rollback to previous config - services restored
Expected Results: Export -> modify -> validate -> sync cycle works. decK catches errors. Rollback restores state.
Feature Checklist: Config as Code, CI/CD Integration (GitOps), Policy Versioning, Rollback, Config Validation

Title: Blue/Green Deployment
Description: Verify zero-downtime upstream switch between blue and green backends
Test Steps:
1. Configure blue upstream + green upstream
2. Switch from blue to green
3. Monitor for dropped requests
4. Verify green handles all traffic
5. Revert to blue seamlessly
Expected Results: 0 dropped requests during switch. Green handles traffic immediately. Can revert.

Title: Claim-based Routing
Description: Verify Kong routes requests based on JWT claims
Test Steps:
1. Create consumer with role=admin claim
2. Configure /admin route to require role=admin
3. Test admin JWT -> /admin 200
4. Test user JWT -> /admin 403
Expected Results: admin JWT: admin route 200. user JWT: admin route 403.

Title: ABAC / RBAC Access Control
Description: Verify role-based access control via JWT claims
Test Steps:
1. Create consumers with role=admin and role=user
2. Configure different permissions per role
3. Test admin -> all routes allowed
4. Test user -> restricted routes 403
Expected Results: admin: full access. user: restricted routes return 403.

Title: Context-based Decision
Description: Verify Kong uses dynamic request context for access decisions
Test Steps:
1. Configure plugin to check transaction amount header
2. TXN > 10000 requires extra auth
3. Test small txn -> passes
4. Test large txn -> blocked without extra auth
Expected Results: Small txn passes. Large txn (>10K) requires additional authorization.

Title: Latency Benchmark
Description: Measure Kong added latency overhead (p50, p95, p99)
Test Steps:
1. Ping demo server directly (no Kong) - baseline
2. Send same request via Kong DP
3. Calculate delta
4. Record p50, p95, p99 latency
Expected Results: Kong overhead < 5ms at p99.

Title: Throughput Test
Description: Verify Kong handles target TPS without resource exhaustion
Test Steps:
1. Ramp up parallel requests from low to target (500 TPS)
2. Monitor for errors at each load level
3. Check Kong CPU and memory on K8s
Expected Results: 0 errors at expected TPS. CPU < 70%, memory stable.
